skill.name: gpt-taste
skill.description: Design prompt skill for creating premium frontend pages with AIDA structure, wide typography, dense bento grids, GSAP motion, and curated image treatments.
skill.summary: Create polished frontend interfaces with structured layout planning, GSAP motion patterns, and strict visual quality checks.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: The reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance findings are false positives from prose and inline markdown examples, not executable code. The only confirmed risk is a hardcoded external image source that generated pages may load from the network. No evidence found of malware, credential access, data exfiltration, or reviewer-targeted prompt injection.

low_findings.title: External Image Source Recommended
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill tells generated UIs to use a picsum.photos image URL. This is a low risk because it creates external network loads in generated output, but it does not send secrets or execute code. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The URL is explicit on line 63, and no credential or user data transmission is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicit on line 63, and no credential or user data transmission is present.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer interpreted markdown backticks and UI examples as Ruby or shell execution. The file is prompt text, and these examples describe Tailwind classes, React components, GSAP names, and verification steps. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The cited lines are natural language or inline markdown examples, with no runnable script file or shell invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural language or inline markdown examples, with no runnable script file or shell invocation.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto And Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The analyzer flagged words such as deterministic seed, randomization, verification, and layout math as weak crypto or reconnaissance. They are design-planning instructions, not cryptographic or system-inspection behavior. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The surrounding context is frontend design guidance, and no filesystem, process, host, or cryptographic API access appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is frontend design guidance, and no filesystem, process, host, or cryptographic API access appears.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Third-Party Asset URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends a third-party image URL for generated pages. Publishers should disclose the runtime network dependency or replace it with user-controlled assets. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The pattern is present at the cited line, but the impact is limited to external asset loading.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present at the cited line, but the impact is limited to external asset loading.

content.value_statement: Many generated pages look repetitive, cramped, and static. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward structured layouts, wide typography, bento grids, and GSAP motion.
actual_capabilities: Requires a design plan before React or UI code is produced.
Guides pages through navigation, hero, feature, motion, and action sections.
Defines hero layout options with strict two to three line heading rules.
Specifies gapless bento grid behavior with dense grid flow.
Recommends GSAP ScrollTrigger, hover physics, text reveals, and card stacking.
Applies bans for meta-labels, weak button contrast, and cramped layouts.
limitations: It is a prompt skill, not a tested component library.
It may recommend third-party placeholder images for generated pages.
It assumes React, Tailwind, GSAP, and related packages are acceptable.
It does not verify final browser rendering by itself.

use_cases.title: Build a Premium Landing Page
use_cases.description: Create a polished AIDA page with a cinematic hero, dense feature section, motion sequence, and clear call to action.

use_cases.title: Improve UI Generation Quality
use_cases.description: Push generated designs away from repeated layouts, cramped headings, empty grid cells, and weak visual hierarchy.

use_cases.title: Add Motion Direction to Frontend Work
use_cases.description: Specify scroll pinning, image scaling, text reveals, and stacked cards before implementation begins.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Landing Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete first draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gpt-taste to create a landing page for a new analytics product. Include hero, features, motion section, pricing, and footer.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh an Existing Page Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for stronger visual structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gpt-taste to redesign this page concept with wider typography, a gapless bento grid, and stronger calls to action.

prompt_templates.title: Plan GSAP Motion Before Coding
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request focused on animation architecture.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gpt-taste to plan and implement a React page with pinned scrolling, image scale transitions, and sequential text reveals.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Distinctive Campaign Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a high-impact production concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use gpt-taste to build a campaign page with randomized layout selection, editorial typography, dense media cards, and a final verification plan.

output_examples.input: Create a homepage for a premium productivity app.
output_examples.output: A design plan with selected layout, typography, motion patterns, bento structure, and a React implementation direction.

output_examples.input: Make this SaaS page feel less generic.
output_examples.output: A revised page concept with wider heading rules, stronger spacing, fewer cards, better contrast, and planned GSAP interactions.

output_examples.input: Add scroll motion to a portfolio page.
output_examples.output: A motion sequence covering pinned sections, image scale transitions, text reveals, and card stacking behavior.

best_practices: Review the generated design plan before accepting the code.
Replace placeholder image URLs with approved project assets when publishing.
Test generated motion on desktop and mobile viewports.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for minimal static pages that should avoid animation dependencies.
Do not publish generated placeholder images without reviewing source and licensing needs.
Do not skip accessibility, responsiveness, and performance checks after generation.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create premium frontend pages with strong layout structure, wide typography, bento grids, and GSAP motion guidance.

faq.question: Does it run Python or shell commands?
faq.answer: No. It asks the assistant to simulate a small randomization step inside the design plan.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The file is prompt text. Static command and crypto findings are false positives, but one external image URL is recommended.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it include finished UI components?
faq.answer: No. It provides instructions that guide an assistant when generating React and Tailwind interface code.

faq.question: What should users review before publishing?
faq.answer: Users should review asset sources, animation performance, accessibility, responsive behavior, and package choices.
